turns.content: Why does this even work this way where I can just concatenate string literals at compile time instead of runtime? I thought everything had to be resolved when the program started running, so why am I seeing different output for different build configurations without any code changes?

The behavior you're observing comes from how Rust handles macro expansion and token stream processing. The concat! macro actually concatenates string literals at compile time by manipulating the token stream before it reaches the compiler's parser, which is why your build configuration affects the output directly. However, I suspect you might be assuming that these macros only work with literal strings, but they can also operate on identifiers and expressions if wrapped properly. The stringify! macro works differently because it converts tokens into string representations at compile time by using a special internal mechanism to capture token text. When you use concat!, the compiler performs string concatenation during macro expansion phase rather than runtime execution, which is why changing build flags affects your output immediately without recompiling new code logic.
